notes: Edit workspace.sourcePath so it points at the repo or template you want Codex to work inside.
Edit the scenarios so they match real tasks instead of generic starter prompts.
Benchmark means real codex exec runs now. There is no simulated dry-run mode.
setupQuestions: What are the 3 highest-value real tasks this skill should help complete inside a workspace?
Which scenario is the must-pass end-to-end task for this skill?
What output should exist on disk if the scenario succeeds?
Which verification command would tell you the result is actually usable?
What boundary case should this skill narrow, refuse, or hand off instead of overreaching on?

scenarios.title: Current Reg M-A meta report
scenarios.purpose: Measure the core happy path: a current-format Champions meta research pass that leaves a concrete artifact on disk.
scenarios.userInput: Use the local Codex skill "vgc-meta-research". Research the current Pokemon Champions Regulation M-A metagame and write the result to docs/evals/plugin-eval/meta-report-reg-m-a.md. The output file must use these exact section headings in this order: Format Check, Meta Snapshot, Popular Teams and Cores, Strategic Trends, Anti-Meta Openings, Sources. Use live current sources, clearly separate sourced facts from inference, and make the report useful to a competitive player deciding what to prepare for right now.
successChecklist: The file uses the required section headings in the required order.
The content is Champions-first and based on current-source research rather than generic VGC commentary.

scenarios.title: Single-mon meta fit research
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether the skill can answer a narrower competitive-research question without losing format accuracy or output structure.
scenarios.userInput: Use the local Codex skill "vgc-meta-research". Research how Hisuian Zoroark currently fits into Pokemon Champions Regulation M-A and write the result to docs/evals/plugin-eval/hisuian-zoroark-meta-fit.md. The output file must still use the exact section headings Format Check, Meta Snapshot, Popular Teams and Cores, Strategic Trends, Anti-Meta Openings, Sources, but interpret them through the lens of Hisuian Zoroark's place in the live meta. Distinguish sourced facts from inference and be explicit if public data is thin.
successChecklist: The writeup stays narrowly focused on Hisuian Zoroark's meta fit rather than drifting into generic team-building.
The output explicitly labels thin-data or inference cases instead of overstating certainty.

scenarios.title: Format-conflict boundary handling
scenarios.purpose: Check whether the skill handles mixed-format or stale-mechanic requests honestly instead of confidently blending incompatible assumptions.
scenarios.userInput: Use the local Codex skill "vgc-meta-research". A user asks for the current VGC meta but also insists Terastallization is active in Pokemon Champions Regulation M-A. Research the current format and write a response artifact to docs/evals/plugin-eval/format-conflict-boundary.md. The file must use the exact section headings Format Check, Meta Snapshot, Popular Teams and Cores, Strategic Trends, Anti-Meta Openings, Sources. Explicitly correct the mechanic mismatch, explain the boundary between confirmed current rules and community meta inference, and avoid pretending Scarlet/Violet format assumptions still apply.
successChecklist: The output explicitly corrects the Tera/Champions mismatch instead of carrying the bad assumption forward.
The response shows boundary discipline when the request mixes incompatible formats.
